summary: Mediterranean wind energy operators face an elevated insider threat risk from privileged access misuse by third-party contractors, compounded by geopolitical uncertainty from North African instability and EU regulatory tightening on component procurement.

lead_indicators.text: Mediterranean geopolitical tensions affect energy infrastructure confidence, disputed maritime zones overlap with offshore wind development areas
North Africa instability creates supply chain disruption risk for Mediterranean wind energy operators dependent on cross-Mediterranean logistics
Iran escalation risk raises Strait of Hormuz energy shipping concerns for Mediterranean operators with Gulf logistics exposure
